name: Nemo facilis vero qu
summary: The uploaded document is not a candidate resume for 'Nemo facilis vero qu'. It is a technical assessment task document from Indus Action outlining tasks for Software Development (REST API development), Database Management (SQL & Schema design), and Data Analysis. As a result, no candidate qualifications, experience, or skills could be evaluated for the Senior Backend Engineer (Node/Mongo) role.
cons: No candidate resume was provided in the uploaded document.
The uploaded document is a technical test instruction sheet.

interviewQuestions.question: Could you please submit your updated resume detailing your experience with Node.js and MongoDB?

resumeText:
Part 1: Software Development Task
● Build a Basic REST API
○ Develop a RESTful API using a language and framework of your choice (e.g., Python with Flask/Django, Node.js with Express, Java with Spring Boot). The API should support the following functionalities:
■ Add Records: Accept JSON input to add a record to the system.
■ Retrieve Records: Allow retrieval of all records, as well as filtering by a specific field (e.g., status or category).
■ Update Records: Enable updating specific fields in a record.
■ Delete Records: Provide the ability to delete a record by ID.
○ Requirements:
■ Use proper coding standards, including comments and error handling.
■ Include basic authentication (e.g., an API key or token-based authentication).
■ Document the API using Swagger or any similar tool.
○ Deliverables:
■ Source code hosted on GitHub (private repository with access granted to the hiring manager).
■ Application hosted on cloud infrastructure/platform of your choice (e.g. Render, Netlify, Heroku)
■ Instructions on how to run the application locally (e.g., README file).

Part 2: Database Management Task
● Database Design and Querying
○ Create a database schema for a basic "Order Management System" that includes:
■ Customers
■ Orders
■ Products
■ Payments
■ Relationships between these entities
■ Populate Data: Insert sample data for 5 customers, 10 orders, and related products and payments.
○ Write SQL queries to:
■ Fetch the top 3 customers with the highest number of orders.
■ Retrieve orders placed in the last 30 days.
■ Calculate total revenue for each product.
● Requirements:
○ Submit the SQL file for schema creation and queries.
○ Provide a brief explanation of your design choices and the purpose of each query.
● Deliverables:
○ SQL script file.
○ Diagram of the database schema (e.g., an ER diagram).

Part 3: Data Analysis Task
● Analyse and Visualise Data
○ You are provided with a dataset. The dataset contains information about user interactions with an e-commerce website, including:
■ User ID
■ Timestamp
■ Action (e.g., viewed_product, added_to_cart, purchased)
■ Product Category
■ Revenue
○ Tasks:
■ Clean and preprocess the data:
● Handle missing values.
● Convert timestamps to a standard format.
● Derive any necessary fields (e.g., session duration, category-wise totals).
■ Perform the following analyses:
● Identify the most popular product categories.
● Calculate the conversion rate (i.e., percentage of purchased actions out of total interactions).
● Visualise user behaviour trends over time (e.g., daily or weekly activity).
● Provide insights and recommendations for improving conversion rates.
○ Requirements:
■ Use Python (e.g., Pandas, Matplotlib/Seaborn) or any other suitable tool.
■ Submit a Jupyter Notebook or equivalent script.
■ Include explanations for each step and insights derived.
○ Deliverables:
■ Python notebook/script with comments and outputs.
■ PDF/HTML export of the notebook for easy review.
